description: You are a backend engineer at a logistics company. A colleague wrote a Python asyncio-based job scheduling service that tracks package routing jobs. The service is broken in production — jobs that should run concurrently are running sequentially, some jobs never complete, and under load the status counters become inconsistent. The project is open in PyCharm at /home/ga/PycharmProjects/async_scheduler. Fix all async/concurrency bugs in the source code under server/ involving async/await correctness, asyncio task creation patterns, and shared mutable state access. Ensure all tests pass without modifying the test files or the public API function signatures.

bug_locations: server/worker.py:process_job — missing await on async_sleep call (blocks event loop)
server/worker.py:run_workers — uses asyncio.coroutine pattern instead of asyncio.gather
server/registry.py:JobRegistry.update_status — not using asyncio.Lock for concurrent updates
server/client.py:fetch_job_status — missing await on aiohttp response.json()
metadata.industry: Computer Systems Design and Related Services
